zplToPdf.title: Free Online ZPL to PDF Converter | ZPL PRO
zplToPdf.h1: Free Online ZPL to PDF Converter
zplToPdf.desc: Convert ZPL code to PDF 4×6 or A4 for free. ZPL PRO online tool for Shopee, Mercado Livre and Zebra printers. 100% in-browser.
zplToPdf.intro: Convert ZPL code to PDF without installing anything. Paste the code directly into the editor, choose the format — 4×6 for thermal printers or A4 for regular printing — and download the PDF in seconds. All processing happens in your browser, without sending data to servers.
zplToPdf.step1: Paste ZPL code into the editor or drag a .zpl file
zplToPdf.step2: Choose the format: PDF 4×6 (thermal label) or PDF A4
zplToPdf.step3: Click Render and download the PDF
zplToPdf.faq1a: ZPL (Zebra Programming Language) is the language used by Zebra thermal printers to create barcode labels, shipping labels and product labels.
zplToPdf.faq2q: Do I need to install anything?
zplToPdf.faq2a: No. The converter works 100% in the browser — Chrome, Firefox, Edge or Safari. No installation or sign-up required.
zplToPdf.faq3q: Does it work with Shopee and Mercado Livre labels?
zplToPdf.faq3a: Yes. It supports ZPL formats generated by Shopee, Mercado Livre, WooCommerce and any system that uses Zebra printers.
zplToPdf.faq4q: What is the label limit per file?
zplToPdf.faq4a: There is no limit. You can convert files with hundreds of labels at once.
zplToPdf.faq5a: Yes. The ZPL code never leaves your device — all processing is local in the browser.

shopeeFix.title: Fix Shopee ZPL Label | ZPL PRO
shopeeFix.desc: Automatically fix oversized Shopee ZPL labels. Free ZPL PRO tool for sellers with Zebra ZD220 and ZD230 printers.
shopeeFix.intro: Automatically fix oversized ZPL labels generated by Shopee. The problem occurs when Shopee's system generates labels with incorrect dimensions — the Zebra printer receives the code but prints at the wrong size or doesn't print at all.
shopeeFix.step1: Paste the Shopee label ZPL code into the editor
shopeeFix.step2: Select your printer DPI (203 DPI for Zebra ZD220/ZD230, 300 DPI for ZD421/ZT230)
shopeeFix.step3: Click Analyze — if there is a problem, click Fix and copy the corrected code
shopeeFix.faq1q: Why does my Shopee label print oversized?
shopeeFix.faq1a: Shopee's system sometimes generates ZPL labels without the correct ^PW and ^LL commands. Without these dimensions, the printer uses its default size, which rarely matches the 100×150mm shipping label paper.
shopeeFix.faq2q: Which printer should I use for Shopee labels?
shopeeFix.faq2a: Zebra ZD220 and ZD230 (203 DPI) printers are the most common among Shopee sellers. If you have a ZD421 or ZT230, select 300 DPI.
shopeeFix.faq3q: Does it work with Mercado Livre labels too?
shopeeFix.faq3a: Yes. The same problem occurs with Mercado Livre, WooCommerce and other marketplaces that use Zebra printers.
shopeeFix.faq4q: Do I need technical knowledge of ZPL?
shopeeFix.faq4a: No. The tool detects and fixes automatically.

validator.title: ZPL Syntax Validator Online | ZPL PRO
validator.desc: Validate and fix errors in ZPL code for free. ZPL PRO tool with line-by-line error detection for Zebra printers.
validator.intro: Validate your ZPL code before printing and avoid wasting paper and time. The validator analyzes the code line by line, detects structural errors, semantic warnings and suggests best practices — with examples of how to fix each issue found.
validator.step1: Paste the ZPL code into the editor
validator.step3: Review errors (red), warnings (yellow) and tips (blue) — each item has an example of how to fix it
validator.faq1q: What causes errors in ZPL code?
validator.faq1a: The most common errors are: missing ^XA or ^XZ, ^FD fields without closing ^FS, and ^FO coordinates outside the print area.
validator.faq2q: What is the difference between an error, warning and tip?
validator.faq2a: Errors (E) prevent printing. Warnings (W) cause unexpected behavior. Tips (I) are best practices that improve compatibility across printers.
validator.faq3q: Does the validator fix errors automatically?
validator.faq3a: Yes, for fixable errors like ^FD without ^FS and negative coordinates. Structural errors require manual correction — the validator explains exactly what to do.
validator.faq4q: Does it work with ZPL generated by ERP systems?
validator.faq4a: Yes. It accepts any ZPL code — SAP, TOTVS, Bling, Tiny or any system that generates Zebra labels.

labelBuilder.title: ZPL Label Builder | ZPLMaster
labelBuilder.desc: Create transport, shipping and logistics labels without writing code. Generate PDF and ZPL ready for printing.

bulkSplitter.title: ZPL Label Batch Splitter | ZPL PRO
bulkSplitter.desc: Split ZPL files with multiple labels into individual files. Free ZPL PRO tool for Shopee, Mercado Livre and fulfillment.
bulkSplitter.intro: Split ZPL files containing hundreds of labels into individual files. Ideal for fulfillment operations that receive massive batches of Shopee or Mercado Livre labels and need to print or archive each label separately.
bulkSplitter.step1: Paste the ZPL code or upload a .zpl file
bulkSplitter.step2: Click Split — the system automatically detects how many labels exist
bulkSplitter.step3: Download the ZIP with labels in ZPL or PDF format
bulkSplitter.faq1q: How many labels can I split at once?
bulkSplitter.faq1a: There is no limit. It has been tested with batches of hundreds of Shopee and Mercado Livre labels without issues.
bulkSplitter.faq2q: Does it work with Shopee labels that have GRF images?
bulkSplitter.faq2a: Yes. The algorithm recognizes the specific format of Shopee labels — including image blocks — and treats each label as a complete unit.
bulkSplitter.faq3q: Can I download the labels as PDF?
bulkSplitter.faq3a: Yes. You can download a ZIP with individual .zpl files or a ZIP with PDFs — one per label.
bulkSplitter.faq4q: Why is my file generating double the number of labels?
bulkSplitter.faq4a: Shopee labels have an internal ^XA^MCY^XZ block. Our tool recognizes this pattern and does not count it as a separate label.

fix.manualNote: Structural errors (E001, E002, E003) require manual correction.
fix.tooltip: Automatically fixes: ^FD fields without ^FS, negative coordinates and missing dimensions

validator.statusValidMsg: No errors or warnings found. The code is ready to use.

errors.E006: Multiple ^XA detected without ^XZ between them — use Bulk Splitter

warnings.W001: Negative coordinates in ^FO — field may be outside the label
warnings.W004: Commands after ^XZ will be ignored by the printer
warnings.W005: ^PQ0 — zero quantity, nothing will be printed

info.I002: No ^FD fields found — label has no content
info.I003: Encoding not declared — printer will use default

generic.explanation: Review this line structure and adjust the commands according to ZPL syntax.

E001.explanation: ^XA starts the label. Without it, the printer cannot parse the block correctly.

E002.explanation: ^XZ ends the label. Without it, the ZPL is incomplete.

E003.explanation: Order is inverted: ^XA must come first and ^XZ must come last.

E004.explanation: Every field opened with ^FD must be closed with ^FS.

E005.explanation: Barcode commands need data (^FD) and a field close (^FS).

E006.explanation: This file contains multiple labels pasted together. Split them before validating individually.
E006.correct: Split each label into a separate block/file

W001.explanation: Negative coordinates can place content outside printable area.

W004.explanation: Anything after ^XZ is ignored by the printer.

W005.explanation: ^PQ0 sets quantity to zero, so nothing is printed.

I001.explanation: Without ^PW/^LL, the printer uses defaults and label size may be wrong.
